preview_text:
The model favors Saudi Arabia at 40%, with Cape Verde given a 29% chance to win. The draw carries 31% probability, making it nearly as likely as a Cape Verde victory in the model's assessment.

The spread suggests a moderately competitive fixture. Saudi Arabia holds a modest edge, but the 11-percentage-point gap between favorite and underdog leaves substantial uncertainty. The draw sitting between the two win probabilities indicates the match could resolve in any of three directions with meaningful probability mass behind each outcome.

The model and sportsbook consensus show material divergence on the underdog. The market assigns Cape Verde 35% to win—a 6-percentage-point lift from the model—while rating the draw 5 points lower at 26%. Saudi Arabia's win probability is nearly identical across both sources at around 39–40%. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the sportsbook consensus, reinforcing the market's tilt toward Cape Verde. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is more accurate.
